completion_promises.criterion_text: src/jarvis/tools/capabilities.py exposes three @tool(parse_docstring=True) functions: list_available_capabilities() -> str, capabilities_refresh() -> str, capabilities_subscribe_updates() -> str.
completion_promises.evidence: Added the three @tool(parse_docstring=True) decorated functions to src/jarvis/tools/capabilities.py and re-exported them via __all__. TestAC001ToolExposure verifies (a) module attribute presence, (b) the BaseTool.invoke method is callable, and (c) tool.name matches the documented function name for each.

Each docstring was authored from API-tools.md §2.1, §2.2, and §2.3 verbatim, including the en-dashes the reasoning model reads. TestAC002DocstringContract spot-checks the contract phrases against tool.description and tool.func.__doc__ (parse_docstring=True strips Returns out of description, so the OK strings and ERROR strings are checked against func.__doc__). The test also asserts the 'stubbed in Phase 2' grep anchor appears in capabilities.py (per swap_point_note).

completion_promises.criterion_text: list_available_capabilities returns JSON-serialised copy of the registry list captured at assemble_tool_list time. Snapshot isolation invariant (ASSUM-006).
completion_promises.evidence: list_available_capabilities captures _capability_registry into a local 'snapshot' reference at call start, then serialises via json.dumps([d.model_dump(mode='json') for d in snapshot]). assemble_tool_list (TASK-J002-015) is documented to assign a fresh list rather than mutate in place, so a concurrent rebinding never affects the in-flight call's snapshot. TestAC003ListReturnsJsonSnapshot verifies JSON shape, the empty-registry case ('[]'), the per-descriptor field round-trip, and that a post-call rebinding to [] does not retro-mutate the JSON returned by an earlier call.

completion_promises.criterion_text: capabilities_refresh returns the exact string 'OK: refresh queued (stubbed in Phase 2 — in-memory registry is always fresh)'.
completion_promises.evidence: Body returns module-level constant _REFRESH_OK_MESSAGE = 'OK: refresh queued (stubbed in Phase 2 — in-memory registry is always fresh)'. TestAC004CapabilitiesRefreshOk asserts byte-exact equality against EXPECTED_REFRESH_OK and idempotency across repeated calls.

completion_promises.criterion_text: capabilities_subscribe_updates returns the exact string 'OK: subscribed (stubbed in Phase 2 — no live updates)'.
completion_promises.evidence: Body returns module-level constant _SUBSCRIBE_OK_MESSAGE = 'OK: subscribed (stubbed in Phase 2 — no live updates)'. TestAC005CapabilitiesSubscribeUpdatesOk asserts byte-exact equality against EXPECTED_SUBSCRIBE_OK and idempotency across repeated calls.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All three never raise; internal errors wrapped as 'ERROR: registry_unavailable — <detail>'.
completion_promises.evidence: Each tool wraps its body in try/except Exception and returns f'ERROR: registry_unavailable — {exc}' on failure (with logger.exception logging full stack). TestAC006NeverRaisesStructuredErrors substitutes a _BoomDescriptor whose model_dump raises RuntimeError into _capability_registry and asserts list_available_capabilities returns a string starting with 'ERROR: registry_unavailable — ' that contains the exception detail; pytest.fail-guarded calls verify all three tools never propagate exceptions.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Concurrent test: list_available_capabilities() and capabilities_refresh() in parallel return the startup snapshot and the OK string respectively, with no mutation between call start and return.
completion_promises.evidence: TestAC007ConcurrentSnapshotIsolation uses threading.Barrier(2) to synchronise the two calls, then asserts (a) refresh returns EXPECTED_REFRESH_OK, (b) the JSON returned by list contains every agent_id from the bound registry, and (c) repeats the race 8 times to catch any non-deterministic mutation. Snapshot isolation is enforced inside list_available_capabilities by capturing _capability_registry into a local reference before iteration.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check src/jarvis/tools/capabilities.py reports 'All checks passed!'. ruff check tests/test_capabilities.py reports a single pre-existing UP017 finding on line 197 (timezone.utc usage in test_last_heartbeat_at_accepts_datetime, untouched by this task and confirmed via git show HEAD:tests/test_capabilities.py). No new lint errors were introduced — the B023 closure-over-loop-variable warnings I initially produced in the 8-iteration concurrent test were resolved by hoisting the helper functions out of the for-loop and passing barrier/results in via Thread args.
